body_txt: Although killing off characters by dropping large objects on them is something normally reserved for cartoons, it still happens occasionally. But the universe (cough, writers, cough) appreciates irony far more than it should , and therefore sees fit that any character who is killed by a falling object will look up and see it just in time to be flattened. They will usually be alerted by a whistling sound , combined with the rapidly growing shadow of the object that is around them. It is rare (but not unknown) for characters to leap out of the way. Usually they instead choose to stare at it in shock until they are crushed. Of course, it's fairly surprising to see a piano bearing down on you in the middle of the sidewalk, and this is very much a case of Truth in Television . Sometimes, a character, if trying to invoke the trope on someone they are fighting against, will also point up slightly to hint that they should look up, to which the enemy inevitably falls for it just as they are flattened. Talking Is a Free Action is in effect here; often, the unfortunate character who's about to be killed is given plenty of time to chat with other characters, think to themselves, or do some expository talking, only to die the second they stop and look up at what's going on, with the danger waiting patiently for them to do so. This makes sense for things like sapient attackers or predatory animals, but it can be unintentionally hilarious in the case of falling objects or automatic weapons and booby traps, which wait just as patiently, as if they were living things! Keep in mind that, as this trope is particularly common in cartoons , where characters are almost impervious to permanent damage, this trope does not always actually result in death. Super-Trope of Falling Chandelier of Doom . Almost always used with Death from Above . Contrast Disney Villain Death .
